steps.title: Use the graph shapes
steps.content: The graph of $f(x)=2^x$ is increasing and curves upward. The graph of $g$ is an increasing line. Since they intersect at $x=h$ and $x=k$, the two graphs are equal at those two endpoints.

steps.title: Compare between intersections
steps.content: A representative model is $y=2^x$ and $y=2x$, which intersect at $x=1$ and $x=2$. Between the two intersections, the line is above the exponential curve; outside them, the exponential curve is above the line.

steps.title: State the interval
steps.content: Thus $g(x)>f(x)$ must occur between the two intersection inputs: $$hD.
